CREATE TABLE timestamps (id INT, ts TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (1, TIMESTAMPTZ '-infinity'), (2, TIMESTAMPTZ '1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC'), (3, TIMESTAMPTZ 'infinity');
SELECT id, isinf(ts) FROM timestamps WHERE ts IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_values AS SELECT id, isinf(ts) AS is_infinite FROM timestamps;
SELECT * FROM test_values WHERE is_infinite = true;
SELECT AVG(ts::TIMESTAMPTZ) OVER (PARTITION BY id) FROM timestamps GROUP BY id HAVING isinf(AVG(ts::TIMESTAMPTZ)) = false;
SELECT id, isinf(rs.ts) FROM (SELECT ts FROM timestamps WHERE ts IS NOT NULL) AS rs;
SELECT t.id, t.ts, isinf(t.ts) FROM timestamps t JOIN (SELECT id FROM timestamps WHERE id = 1) AS sub ON t.id = sub.id;

CREATE TABLE extended_values AS SELECT t1.id, isinf(t1.ts) AS is_infinite, isinf(t2.ts) AS is_infinite_joined FROM timestamps t1 JOIN timestamps t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE isinf(t1.ts) OR isinf(t2.ts);
SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM timestamps WHERE isinf(ts));
